title: atalantia citrifolia
intro: Atalantia citrifolia, also known as the Calamondin or Wild Lime, is a tropical plant species native to Southeast Asia and India, belonging to the family Rutaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is known to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems. Atalantia citrifolia can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and juices, as well as in traditional medicine, where it is often used to treat various ailments.
